_note: Updated 2026-05-29 cycle AM overnight-triage. Critic participated tonight via the closing METRIC_INTEGRITY_PROTOCOL § 3.1 attestation AND a substantive independent verification. Tonight was a heartbeat-only cycle (FIQ + bug-reports inbox both ABSENT for the 73rd consecutive cycle, verified by directory-absence checks not waved off), so the three § 3.1 concrete questions resolve N/A by absence: no bug-report diagnoses to scrutinize, no proposals to challenge, no FIQ grades to audit. Cycle AM had a genuine NEGATIVE app-health movement (88.1 -> 86.6, A- holds) -- the symmetric inverse of cycle AL's positive A12 settle. On a negative-movement night the failure mode flips: the easy spin is either (a) PANIC ('app-health is regressing, something broke!') or (b) DEFLECTION ('not my problem, ignore it'). The critic guarded against BOTH and demanded the same surfaced-vs-caused rigor regardless of sign. Independent verification performed: confirmed exactly ONE dimension moved by reading the app-health.html diff verbatim -- A12_operational 90->60 (now status yellow, label '8 recent skip-dirty', pipeline green->red); A8_performance HELD at 83 (cycle-AH perf gain still sustained); the other 10 dims held byte-identical (A1 80, A2 100, A3 98, A4 93, A5 88, A6 92, A7 100, A9 95, A10 100, A11 88); attention_items + agent_attention re-populated with the single A12 skip-dirty item (founder_attention still empty). The critic then DEMANDED surfaced-vs-caused proof: like cycle AL the regen PRODUCED the new number (86.6) while HEAD still commits 88.1 (`git show HEAD:docs/reports/app-health.html` = overall_score 88.1 / A12 90) -- which superficially looks CAUSED -- but A12's inputs (downloads-watcher logs + approvals-pipeline.json) are written by the CONCURRENT watcher cron, NOT regen; aggregate-app-health.py only READS them, so the degradation was ALREADY in the watcher logs before the 20:02 run -> SURFACED not CAUSED. The critic GROUNDED the 8/10 by demanding the last-10 watcher logs verbatim: 8 skip-dirty (20:00/19:55/19:50/19:45/19:40/19:35/19:30/19:25Z) + 2 CLEAN (19:20/19:15Z), and crucially verified the skip-dirty FILES are EXACTLY the two pre-existing dirty working-tree items at run-start (.claude/state/emu-unified-2026-05-29.log + .pw-full-sweep.log = concurrent emulator/playwright-sweep artifacts). KEY CROSS-SIGNAL CONSISTENCY the critic verified: unlike cycle AK's yellow-vs-red split and unlike AL's both-green, THIS cycle BOTH signals coherently agree the watcher is BLOCKED -- approvals-pipeline.json (as_of 20:00:48Z) status=red, summary='watcher cycling · applies blocked (8 skips on dirty tree) · 1 in inbox'. The critic's crucial honesty move (inverse of AL's 'don't call a settle a permanent fix'): don't call this oscillation a REGRESSION or a DEFECT -- A12 is yellow because the tree is CURRENTLY dirty with concurrent artifacts, exactly as cycle AL PREDICTED it would re-oscillate; it will re-settle green once those artifacts are committed/gitignored. The watcher skip-dirty is a safety feature working as designed; resolution = clearing the two concurrent artifacts (Founder/concurrent-process territory); manufacturing a proposal = ship-count gaming per Rule 2. The critic confirmed the commit used an explicit pathspec capturing ONLY this cycle's own files and did NOT sweep the two concurrent dirty artifacts into the commit. The critic actively guarded AGAINST a false-panic regression claim on a negative night, against deflecting a real surfaced signal, AND against manufacturing busywork -- rather than rubber-stamping. Critic token counter at ~174k cumulative since last rest (threshold crossed at cycle AH; +~14k this cycle); status remains active and no rest triggered -- heartbeat-only critic load is genuinely light per-cycle. Critic attests cleanly: substantive heartbeat cycle, ship closes.
